title: Mixtral 8x22B
description: A larger open-weights Mixtral sparse mixture-of-experts model with 141 billion total parameters, about 39 billion active per token, and a 64,000-token context window under Apache 2.0.
openingSummary: Mixtral 8x22B is Mistral AI's larger sparse mixture-of-experts open-weights release. A router activates a subset of expert feed-forward blocks per token, so the model stores 141 billion total parameters while keeping active compute closer to a 39-billion-parameter dense stack.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: Mixtral 8x22B is a decoder-only sparse mixture-of-experts language model from Mistral AI, released on 2024-04-17 in the Cheaper, Better, Faster, Stronger announcement. Mistral describes it as a sparse mixture-of-experts model that uses only 39 billion active parameters out of 141 billion total, offering higher capacity than Mixtral 8x7B while keeping sparse activation. The release ships under the Apache 2.0 license as open weights.

inputsAndOutputs.title: Inputs And Outputs
inputsAndOutputs.body: Mixtral 8x22B is a text-in, text-out model. Official release materials list English, French, Italian, German, and Spanish support, a 64,000-token context window, and strong mathematics and coding behavior in the base checkpoint. This page keeps those figures because they are the conservative operating points repeated across Mistral's release post and changelog.

architecture.title: Architecture
architecture.body: As a decoder-only sparse mixture-of-experts model, Mixtral 8x22B keeps the familiar transformer decoder skeleton but replaces each dense feed-forward block with routed experts at larger per-expert capacity than Mixtral 8x7B. Mistral's release post emphasizes sparse activation patterns that make inference faster than dense models of comparable capability while storing a much larger total parameter budget. The diagram below stays at the decoder level—attention, normalization, and a sparse expert routing block—so it teaches token-to-router-to-selected-experts flow and capacity-versus-activation tradeoffs without turning every internal block name into an extra claim.

importantModules.title: Important Modules
importantModules.body: The registry-backed modules below are the smallest set worth opening first when you want to connect this Mixtral page to the site's module reference. Mixture-of-experts routing is the defining feed-forward behavior here, while grouped-query attention, RMS normalization, SwiGLU, and rotary position encoding anchor the high-level stack shown above.

training.title: Training
training.body: Mistral's release post positions Mixtral 8x22B as a continuation of the open Mixtral family with stronger reasoning, multilingual, and coding behavior than earlier releases. The same announcement also releases an instruction-tuned Mixtral 8x22B Instruct variant, but this page does not restate fine-tuning recipe details beyond what those primary sources publish.

practicalNotes.title: Practical Notes
practicalNotes.body: Read Mixtral 8x22B as the higher-capacity sparse expert release in the Mixtral line. Public materials cite 141 billion total parameters with about 39 billion active per token and a 64,000-token context window, which is the main practical distinction when you compare memory residency, recall from long documents, and inference cost against Mixtral 8x7B or a dense model with a similar total parameter budget.

architectureGraph.alt: Mixtral 8x22B architecture diagram with token embeddings, rotary position encoding, a repeated decoder block containing grouped-query attention, RMSNorm, and a sparse expert routing block that activates a subset of larger experts per token, then an output projection and softmax.

Output
Probabilities
outputProbabilities.summary: The model's probability distribution over the next token

softmax.label: Softmax
softmax.summary: Turns final vocabulary scores into probabilities

linear.label:
Output
Linear
linear.summary: Projects the final hidden state into vocabulary logits

decoderStack.summary: Repeated decoder block container for the Mixtral 8x22B stack

repeatMarker.label: N×
repeatMarker.summary: This decoder block repeats through the full stack

rmsnormAttention.label: RMSNorm
rmsnormAttention.summary: Normalizes the residual stream before the attention step

decoderAttention.label:
Grouped-Query
Attention
decoderAttention.summary: Each token reads earlier context under the model's attention mask

rmsnormMoe.label: RMSNorm
rmsnormMoe.summary: Normalizes the stream before the sparse expert routing block

moeRouting.label:
Expert
Router
moeRouting.summary: Activates a sparse subset of larger feed-forward experts per token and combines their outputs

ropeInject.summary: Applies token states together with rotary position information before the stack

rope.label: RoPE
rope.summary: Rotary position encoding gives the attention stack token order information

inputEmbedding.label:
Input
Embedding
inputEmbedding.summary: Maps token IDs into hidden vectors
